revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:May 13 (old lunar calendar)]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of May 13 (old lunar calendar)]]</includeonly>

'''旧暦5月13日'''は[[旧暦]][[5月 (旧暦)|5月]]の13日目である。六曜は[[六曜#大安|大安]]である。
== できごと == * [[寛平]]元年（[[889年]]_月_日） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（[[1401年]]_月_日） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[1672年]]_月_日） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（[[1818年]]_月_日） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（[[1866年]]_月_日） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]_月_日） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置
== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]_月_日） - [[江川坦庵]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[文政]]12年（[[1829年]]_月_日） - [[松平定信]]、江戸幕府老中・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
{{msg:旧暦360日}} *前日[[5月12日 (旧暦)|5月12日]] 翌日[[5月14日 (旧暦)|5月14日]] - 前月[[4月13日 (旧暦)|4月13日]] 翌月[[6月13日 (旧暦)|6月13日]] *新暦[[5月13日]]
{{msg:stub}}

'''旧暦5月13日'''は[[旧暦]][[5月 (旧暦)|5月]]の13日目である。六曜は[[六曜#大安|大安]]である。
== できごと == * [[寛平]]元年（[[889年]]_月_日） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（[[1401年]]_月_日） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[1672年]]_月_日） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（[[1818年]]_月_日） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（[[1866年]]_月_日） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]_月_日） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置
== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]_月_日） - [[江川坦庵]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[文政]]12年（[[1829年]]_月_日） - [[松平定信]]、江戸幕府老中・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
{{旧暦360日}} *前日[[5月12日 (旧暦)|5月12日]] 翌日[[5月14日 (旧暦)|5月14日]] - 前月[[4月13日 (旧暦)|4月13日]] 翌月[[6月13日 (旧暦)|6月13日]] *新暦[[5月13日]]
{{stub}}

revision.text:
{{旧暦5月}} '''旧暦5月13日'''は[[5月 (旧暦)|旧暦5月]]の13日目である。六曜は[[六曜#大安|大安]]である。
== できごと == * [[寛平]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[889年]][[6月14日]]） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1401年]][[6月24日]]） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1672年]][[6月8日]]） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1818年]][[6月16日]]） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[6月25日]]） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[6月22日]]） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置 * 明治3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[6月11日]]） - [[庚午事変]]
== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[文政]]12年（グレゴリオ暦[[1829年]]6月14日） - [[松平定信]]、[[江戸幕府]][[老中]]・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}

== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）

== 記念日・年中行事 == *竹酔日（ちくすいび） - 中国の俗説で、[[竹]]を[[移植 (生物)|移植]]するのに最良とされる日。この日は竹が酔っているので、移植されたことがわからず、よく根付くという。竹迷日、竜生日、竹誕日などともいう。

== できごと == * [[寛平]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[889年]][[6月14日]]） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1401年]][[6月24日]]） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1672年]][[6月8日]]） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1818年]][[6月16日]]） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[6月25日]]） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[6月22日]]） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置 * 明治3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[6月11日]]） - [[庚午事変]]
== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[文政]]12年（グレゴリオ暦[[1829年]]6月14日） - [[松平定信]]、[[江戸幕府]][[老中]]・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 == *竹酔日（ちくすいび） - 中国の俗説で、[[竹]]を[[移植 (生物)|移植]]するのに最良とされる日。この日は竹が酔っているので、移植されたことがわからず、よく根付くという。竹迷日、竜生日、竹誕日などともいう。
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}
[[Category:japanese lunar calendar]][[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese Lunar Calendar]] [[Category:Lunar May]][[sn:May 13日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]]

== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）

{{Lunar May}} {{Lunar May}} '''May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)''' is the DAYX day of month {{ill|May (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the tradional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}}.
==OVERVIEW== {{Lunar May}} ''May 13'''<!--Lunar May13--> is the Nth day of 、[[5月 (旧暦)|Lunar May]]の13日目である。六曜は[[六曜#大安|大安]][[Category:大安]]である。
== Events == * [[寛平]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[889年]][[6月14日]]） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1401年]][[6月24日]]） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1672年]][[6月8日]]） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1818年]][[6月16日]]） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[6月25日]]） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[6月22日]]） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置 * 明治3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[6月11日]]） - [[庚午事変]]
== Births == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== Deaths == * [[文政]]12年（グレゴリオ暦[[1829年]]6月14日） - [[松平定信]]、[[江戸幕府]][[老中]]・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== Commemorations == *竹酔日（ちくすいび） - 中国の俗説で、[[竹]]を[[移植 (生物)|移植]]するのに最良とされる日。この日は竹が酔っているので、移植されたことがわからず、よく根付くという。竹迷日、竜生日、竹誕日などともいう。
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}
[[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category:Lunar May]][[sn:May 13日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] {{citation needed}}
{{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[Category:大安]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]][[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[Category:not Butsumetsu]]

<!--== {{Lunar May}} ''May 13'''<!--Lunar May13--> is the Nth day of 、[[5月 (旧暦)|Lunar May]]の13日目である。六曜は[[六曜#大安|大安]][[Category:大安]]である。
--> == Events == * [[寛平]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[889年]][[6月14日]]） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1401年]][[6月24日]]） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1672年]][[6月8日]]） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1818年]][[6月16日]]） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[6月25日]]） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[6月22日]]） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置 * 明治3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[6月11日]]） - [[庚午事変]]
== Births == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== Deaths == * [[文政]]12年（グレゴリオ暦[[1829年]]6月14日） - [[松平定信]]、[[江戸幕府]][[老中]]・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== Commemorations == *竹酔日（ちくすいび） - 中国の俗説で、[[竹]]を[[移植 (生物)|移植]]するのに最良とされる日。この日は竹が酔っているので、移植されたことがわからず、よく根付くという。竹迷日、竜生日、竹誕日などともいう。
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}

--> == Events == * [[寛平]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[889年]][[6月14日]]） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1401年]][[6月24日]]） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1672年]][[6月8日]]） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1818年]][[6月16日]]） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[6月25日]]） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[6月22日]]） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置 * 明治3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[6月11日]]） - [[庚午事変]]
== Births == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== Deaths == * [[文政]]12年（グレゴリオ暦[[1829年]]6月14日） - [[松平定信]]、[[江戸幕府]][[老中]]・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== Commemorations == *竹酔日（ちくすいび） - 中国の俗説で、[[竹]]を[[移植 (生物)|移植]]するのに最良とされる日。この日は竹が酔っているので、移植されたことがわからず、よく根付くという。竹迷日、竜生日、竹誕日などともいう。
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}
[[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category:Lunar May]][[sn:May 13日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] {{citation needed}}

revision.text:
[[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}}.
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}'s great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}.
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|応永}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}}) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}}) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}}.
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}}) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}}) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}}) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}}) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}}, military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}}, senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|寛政の改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}}).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

[[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}}.
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}'s great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}.
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|応永}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}}) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}}) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}}.
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}}) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}}) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}}) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}}) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}}, military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}}, senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|寛政の改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}}).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

revision.text:
[[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}}.
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}'s great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}.
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|応永}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}}) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}}) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}}.
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}}) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}}) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}}) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}}) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}}, military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}}, senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|寛政の改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}}).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}'s great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}.

* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}}) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}}.
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}}) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}}) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}}) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}}) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}}, military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}}, senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|寛政の改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}}).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. [[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}}.
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}}) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}}'s great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}.
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|応永}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}}) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}}) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}}.
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}}) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}}) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}}) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}}) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}}, military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}}).

* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

[[Category:Taian]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]] [[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]] [[Category:Days 13 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[Category:null category]] [[Category:Untranslated Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category: Lunar Calendar Days]] [[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]

[[sn:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .

* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.

== Births ==

== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).

* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

revision.text:
[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.

* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

[[Category:Taian]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]] [[Category:Days 13 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:Untranslated Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category: Lunar Calendar Days]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]]

[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401年}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801年}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

[[Category:Taian]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]] [[Category:Days 13 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:Untranslated Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category: Lunar Calendar Days]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]]

[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year }} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year }} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year }} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.

* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

[[Category:Taian]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:Days 13 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[Category:Untranslated Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category: Lunar Calendar Days]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]]

[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year }} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year }} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year }} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year }} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year }} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year }} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year }} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year }} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year }} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year }} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year }} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

revision.text:
[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year }} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year }} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year }} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year }} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year }} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year }} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year }} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year }} ).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year }} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Japanese Traditional Lunar Months" to "Japanese Traditional Lunisolar Months"
revision.text:
[[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year }} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year }} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year }} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year }} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year }} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year }} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year }} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year }} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year }} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---
