* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=draft|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|12=draft|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

{{draft categories| [[Category:Historical events in Japan]] [[Category:Economic history of Japan]] [[Category:Japanese diplomatic history]] [[Category:Publishing history]] [[Category:Shogunate policies]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar May]] [[Category:Taian]] [[Category:Days 13 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Lunisolar]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:寛平1st yearの出来事]] [[sn:May 13日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:ay 13 (Lunisolar)]]

[[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=draft|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|12=draft|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

[[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=draft|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

revision.text:
[[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:May 13 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:May 13 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:May 13 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-yue:五月十三]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}

* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* **Bamboo Drunken Day** (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as **Bamboo Bewilderment Day** (竹迷日), **Dragon Birth Day** (竜生日), or **Bamboo Birth Day** (竹誕日).
---

It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. Text replacement - "|1=Julian calendar|" to "|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|"
Text replacement - "|明治|" to "|明治|1=Meiji era|"
getting rid of this category. It is not useful
doing this because it is quite important for standardization in my corrections system
revision.text:
{{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|qq=}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|qq=}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).
--- == See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513|qq=}}
==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|qq=}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|qq=}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:5月13日 (旧暦) up to rev 97894749
revision.text:
{{旧暦5月}} '''旧暦5月13日'''は、[[5月 (旧暦)|旧暦5月]]の13日目である。六曜は[[六曜#大安|大安]]である。
== できごと == * [[寛平]]元年（[[ユリウス暦]][[889年]][[6月14日]]） - [[桓武天皇]]の曾孫・高望王が平姓を賜る。[[平将門]]の祖父 * [[応永]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1401年]][[6月24日]]） - [[足利義満]]が遣明使を派遣。日明貿易への第一歩 * [[寛文]]12年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1672年]][[6月8日]]） - 小石川の水戸藩邸に彰考館を開設。『[[大日本史]]』の編輯が本格化 * [[文政]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1818年]][[6月16日]]） - イギリス人ゴードンが浦賀に来航し貿易を求める。江戸幕府は拒否 * [[慶応]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1866年]][[6月25日]]） - 江戸幕府が英仏米蘭と輸入税引き下げの「[[改税約書]]（江戸協約）」を締結 * [[明治]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[6月22日]]） - 「出版条令」制定。出版の許可制と出版取調所を設置 * 明治3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[6月11日]]） - [[庚午事変]]
== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[文政]]12年（グレゴリオ暦[[1829年]]6月14日） - [[松平定信]]、[[江戸幕府]][[老中]]・[[寛政の改革]]を実施（* [[1758年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 == *竹酔日（ちくすいび） - 中国の俗説で、[[竹]]を[[移植 (生物)|移植]]するのに最良とされる日。この日は竹が酔っているので、移植されたことがわからず、よく根付くという。竹迷日、竜生日、竹誕日などともいう。
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[5月13日 (旧暦)]] to [[May 13 (Lunisolar)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

== 誕生日 == * [[享和]]元年（グレゴリオ暦[[1801年]][[6月23日]]） - [[江川英龍]]、伊豆韮山代官・兵学者（+ [[1855年]]）

== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513}}

* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|12=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|12=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=|12=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|12=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=|12=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|12=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|12=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|12=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|12=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|12=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|12=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|12=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|12=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=|12=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|12=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=|12=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).
--- == See Also ==

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|qq=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=|qq=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|qq=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=|qq=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=|qq=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|qq=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|qq=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=|qq=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==

It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=|qq=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|qq=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|qq=draft}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=|qq=draft}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|qq=draft}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=|qq=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|qq=draft}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=|qq=draft}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=|qq=draft}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|qq=draft}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|qq=draft}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|qq=draft}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|qq=draft}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=|qq=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==

It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō (Japan)|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1866|qq=}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 25|qq=}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 22|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 11|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōwa (Japan)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1801|qq=}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egawa Hidetatsu|qq=}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1855|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1829|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Sadanobu|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1758|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1829|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Sadanobu|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1758|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Japanese diplomatic history]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpyō (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpyō (era)|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/889|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu|qq=}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1401|qq=}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 24|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun (era)|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1672|qq=}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|qq=}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Nihonshi|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1818|qq=}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|qq=}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uraga, Kanagawa|qq=}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō (Japan)|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1866|qq=}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 25|qq=}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 22|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 11|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōwa (Japan)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1801|qq=}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egawa Hidetatsu|qq=}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1855|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1829|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Sadanobu|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1758|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).
--- == See Also ==

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpyō (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpyō (era)|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|889|ja|889th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/889|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu|qq=}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|ja|1401st year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1401|qq=}} {{ill|June 24|ja|6月24日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 24|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|ja|足利義満|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qq=}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun (era)|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672nd year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1672|qq=}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|qq=}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Nihonshi|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1818|qq=}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|qq=}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uraga, Kanagawa|qq=}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō (Japan)|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1866|qq=}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 25|qq=}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 22|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 11|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōwa (Japan)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1801|qq=}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egawa Hidetatsu|qq=}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1855|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1829|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Sadanobu|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758th year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1758|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|ja|寛文|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun (era)|qq=}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Gregorian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|1672|ja|1672年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1672|qq=}} {{ill|June 8|ja|6月8日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 8|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|qq=}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|ja|大日本史|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Nihonshi|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|ja|1818年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1818|qq=}} {{ill|June 16|ja|6月16日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 16|qq=}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|ja|浦賀|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uraga, Kanagawa|qq=}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō (Japan)|ja|慶応|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō (Japan)|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|ja|1866年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1866|qq=}} {{ill|June 25|ja|6月25日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 25|qq=}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|ja|1869年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1869|qq=}} {{ill|June 22|ja|6月22日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 22|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|ja|1870年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}} {{ill|June 11|ja|6月11日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 11|qq=}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa (Japan)|ja|享和|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōwa (Japan)|qq=}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|ja|1801st year|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1801|qq=}} {{ill|June 23|ja|6月23日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 23|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|ja|江川英龍|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egawa Hidetatsu|qq=}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|ja|1855年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1855|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei (era)|ja|文政|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei (era)|qq=}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|ja|1829年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1829|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|ja|松平定信|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Sadanobu|qq=}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* {{ill|1758|ja|1758年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1758|qq=}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpyō (era)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpyō (era)|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Julian calendar|qq=}} {{ill|889|ja|889年|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/889|qq=}} {{ill|June 14|ja|6月14日|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 14|qq=}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Kanmu|ja|桓武天皇|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu|qq=}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} .

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 1 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:889|889]] [[:en:June 14|June 14]] ) – Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Julian [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:June 24|June 24]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun (era)]] 12 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1672|1672]] [[:en:June 8|June 8]] ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|qq=}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]] .
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1818|1818]] [[:en:June 16|June 16]] ) – British trader Gordon arrives at [[:en:Uraga, Kanagawa|Uraga, Kanagawa]] seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (Japan)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:June 25|June 25]] ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:June 22|June 22]] ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:June 11|June 11]] ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* [[:en:Kyōwa|Kyōwa (Japan)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1801|1801]] [[:en:June 23|June 23]] ) – [[:en:Egawa Hidetatsu|Egawa Hidetatsu]] , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ [[:en:1855|1855]] ).
== Deaths ==
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 12 (Gregorian [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:June 14|June 14]] ) – [[:en:Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* [[:en:1758|1758]] ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

[[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[sn:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 13일]] [[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月十三]] [[en:draft:May 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:寛平1st yearのEvents]] [[sn:May 13日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:ay 13 (Lunisolar)]] [[ar:May 13 (Lunisolar)]]

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|qq=}} .
== Events ==
* [[:en:Kanpyō (era)|Kanpyō]] 1 ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:889|889]] [[:en:June 14|June 14]] ) – Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kanmu|Kanmu]] 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei (Japan)|qq=}} 8 (Julian [[:en:1401|1401]] [[:en:June 24|June 24]] ) – [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun (era)]] 12 ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1672|1672]] [[:en:June 8|June 8]] ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|qq=}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]] .
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1818|1818]] [[:en:June 16|June 16]] ) – British trader Gordon arrives at [[:en:Uraga, Kanagawa|Uraga, Kanagawa]] seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} refuses.
* [[:en:Keiō|Keiō (Japan)]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1866|1866]] [[:en:June 25|June 25]] ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|qq=}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 (Gregorian [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:June 22|June 22]] ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|qq=}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:June 11|June 11]] ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|qq=}} occurs.
== Births ==
* [[:en:Kyōwa|Kyōwa (Japan)]] 1 (Gregorian [[:en:1801|1801]] [[:en:June 23|June 23]] ) – [[:en:Egawa Hidetatsu|Egawa Hidetatsu]] , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ [[:en:1855|1855]] ).
== Deaths ==
* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 12 (Gregorian [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:June 14|June 14]] ) – [[:en:Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* [[:en:1758|1758]] ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

* [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei (era)]] 12 (Gregorian [[:en:1829|1829]] [[:en:June 14|June 14]] ) – [[:en:Matsudaira Sadanobu|Matsudaira Sadanobu]] , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|qq=}} (* [[:en:1758|1758]] ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).
