<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 13일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月十三]][[zh:五月十三]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 13일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月十三]][[zh:五月十三]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 13 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月13日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 13일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 13 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月十三]][[zh:五月十三]]
{{Not to be confused with|May 13|qq=May 13}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|889|en|889|WD=Q23833|lt=889}} {{ill|June 14|en|June 14|WD=Q2612|lt=June 14}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Kanmu}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|en|1401|WD=Q6110|lt=1401}} {{ill|June 24|en|June 24|WD=Q2665|lt=June 24}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1672|en|1672|WD=Q7614|lt=1672}} {{ill|June 8|en|June 8|WD=Q2621|lt=June 8}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|1=Shōkōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488062}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihonshi}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|en|1818|WD=Q6964|lt=1818}} {{ill|June 16|en|June 16|WD=Q2653|lt=June 16}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|en|Uraga, Kanagawa|WD=Q2735604|lt=Uraga, Kanagawa}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō (Japan)}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|en|1866|WD=Q7712|lt=1866}} {{ill|June 25|en|June 25|WD=Q2662|lt=June 25}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|1=Tariff Convention|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498864}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} {{ill|June 22|en|June 22|WD=Q2663|lt=June 22}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} {{ill|June 11|en|June 11|WD=Q2617|lt=June 11}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|1=Kōgo Jihen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485995}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa|en|Kyōwa|WD=Q960763|lt=Kyōwa (Japan)}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|en|1801|WD=Q6877|lt=1801}} {{ill|June 23|en|June 23|WD=Q2668|lt=June 23}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|en|Egawa Hidetatsu|WD=Q366267|lt=Egawa Hidetatsu}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|en|1855|WD=Q7673|lt=1855}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} {{ill|June 14|en|June 14|WD=Q2612|lt=June 14}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|en|Matsudaira Sadanobu|WD=Q284038|lt=Matsudaira Sadanobu}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (* {{ill|1758|en|1758|WD=Q7639|lt=1758}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).
--- == See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|12|5|14|4|13|6|5|0513|qq=}}

The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō (Japan)}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|en|1866|WD=Q7712|lt=1866}} {{ill|June 25|en|June 25|WD=Q2662|lt=June 25}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|1=Tariff Convention|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498864}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} {{ill|June 22|en|June 22|WD=Q2663|lt=June 22}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} {{ill|June 11|en|June 11|WD=Q2617|lt=June 11}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|1=Kōgo Jihen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485995}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa|en|Kyōwa|WD=Q960763|lt=Kyōwa (Japan)}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|en|1801|WD=Q6877|lt=1801}} {{ill|June 23|en|June 23|WD=Q2668|lt=June 23}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|en|Egawa Hidetatsu|WD=Q366267|lt=Egawa Hidetatsu}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|en|1855|WD=Q7673|lt=1855}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829}} {{ill|June 14|en|June 14|WD=Q2612|lt=June 14}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|en|Matsudaira Sadanobu|WD=Q284038|lt=Matsudaira Sadanobu}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (* {{ill|1758|en|1758|WD=Q7639|lt=1758}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).
---

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月十三|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 13일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 13''' is the 13th day of month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844|1=taian}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|889|en|889|WD=Q23833|lt=889|1=889}} {{ill|June 14|en|June 14|WD=Q2612|lt=June 14|1=June 14}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|WD=Q314846|lt=Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|en|1401|WD=Q6110|lt=1401|1=1401}} {{ill|June 24|en|June 24|WD=Q2665|lt=June 24|1=June 24}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|WD=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun (era)|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1672|en|1672|WD=Q7614|lt=1672|1=1672}} {{ill|June 8|en|June 8|WD=Q2621|lt=June 8|1=June 8}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|1=Shōkōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488062}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihonshi|1=Dai Nihonshi}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)|1=Bunsei}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|en|1818|WD=Q6964|lt=1818|1=1818}} {{ill|June 16|en|June 16|WD=Q2653|lt=June 16|1=June 16}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|en|Uraga, Kanagawa|WD=Q2735604|lt=Uraga, Kanagawa|1=Uraga, Kanagawa}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō (Japan)|1=Keiō}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|en|1866|WD=Q7712|lt=1866|1=1866}} {{ill|June 25|en|June 25|WD=Q2662|lt=June 25|1=June 25}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|1=Tariff Convention|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498864}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} {{ill|June 22|en|June 22|WD=Q2663|lt=June 22|1=June 22}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} {{ill|June 11|en|June 11|WD=Q2617|lt=June 11|1=June 11}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|1=Q11485995|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485995}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa|en|Kyōwa|WD=Q960763|lt=Kyōwa (Japan)|1=Kyōwa}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|en|1801|WD=Q6877|lt=1801|1=1801}} {{ill|June 23|en|June 23|WD=Q2668|lt=June 23|1=June 23}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|en|Egawa Hidetatsu|WD=Q366267|lt=Egawa Hidetatsu|1=Egawa Hidetatsu}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|en|1855|WD=Q7673|lt=1855|1=1855}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)|1=Bunsei}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|en|1829|WD=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}} {{ill|June 14|en|June 14|WD=Q2612|lt=June 14|1=June 14}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|en|Matsudaira Sadanobu|WD=Q284038|lt=Matsudaira Sadanobu|1=Matsudaira Sadanobu}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (* {{ill|1758|en|1758|WD=Q7639|lt=1758|1=1758}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).

It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|qid=Q96759844|1=taian}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1306255}} .
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|qid=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|889|en|889|qid=Q23833|lt=889|1=889}} {{ill|June 14|en|June 14|qid=Q2612|lt=June 14|1=June 14}} ) – Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} 's great-grandson, Takamochi-ō, is granted the surname Taira, becoming the ancestor of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} .
* {{ill|Ōei (Japan)|ja|Ōei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōei (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1401|en|1401|qid=Q6110|lt=1401|1=1401}} {{ill|June 24|en|June 24|qid=Q2665|lt=June 24|1=June 24}} ) – {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|en|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|qid=Q248381|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}} dispatches an envoy to Ming China, marking the beginning of official Japan-Ming trade relations.
* {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun (era)|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1672|en|1672|qid=Q7614|lt=1672|1=1672}} {{ill|June 8|en|June 8|qid=Q2621|lt=June 8|1=June 8}} ) – The {{ill|Shōkōkan|ja|彰考館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkōkan|1=Shōkōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488062}} is established at the Mito Domain residence in Koishikawa, advancing the compilation of the {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|qid=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihonshi|1=Dai Nihonshi}} .
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)|1=Bunsei}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1818|en|1818|qid=Q6964|lt=1818|1=1818}} {{ill|June 16|en|June 16|qid=Q2653|lt=June 16|1=June 16}} ) – British trader Gordon arrives at {{ill|Uraga, Kanagawa|en|Uraga, Kanagawa|qid=Q2735604|lt=Uraga, Kanagawa|1=Uraga, Kanagawa}} seeking trade relations. The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refuses.
* {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|qid=Q776775|lt=Keiō (Japan)|1=Keiō}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1866|en|1866|qid=Q7712|lt=1866|1=1866}} {{ill|June 25|en|June 25|qid=Q2662|lt=June 25|1=June 25}} ) – The Tokugawa shogunate signs the {{ill|Kaizei Yakusho|ja|改税約書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaizei Yakusho|1=Tariff Convention|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498864}} (Edo Treaty) with Britain, France, the United States, and the Netherlands, lowering import tariffs.
* {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 (Gregorian {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} {{ill|June 22|en|June 22|qid=Q2663|lt=June 22|1=June 22}} ) – The {{ill|Shuppan Jōrei|ja|出版条令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuppan Jōrei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Publication Ordinance) is enacted, requiring government approval for publications and establishing the Publishing Investigation Office.
* Meiji 3 (Gregorian {{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} {{ill|June 11|en|June 11|qid=Q2617|lt=June 11|1=June 11}} ) – The {{ill|Kōgo Jihen|ja|庚午事変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Jihen|1=Q11485995|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485995}} occurs.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Kyōwa|en|Kyōwa|qid=Q960763|lt=Kyōwa (Japan)|1=Kyōwa}} 1 (Gregorian {{ill|1801|en|1801|qid=Q6877|lt=1801|1=1801}} {{ill|June 23|en|June 23|qid=Q2668|lt=June 23|1=June 23}} ) – {{ill|Egawa Hidetatsu|en|Egawa Hidetatsu|qid=Q366267|lt=Egawa Hidetatsu|1=Egawa Hidetatsu}} , military scholar and magistrate of Nirayama in Izu Province (+ {{ill|1855|en|1855|qid=Q7673|lt=1855|1=1855}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei (era)|1=Bunsei}} 12 (Gregorian {{ill|1829|en|1829|qid=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}} {{ill|June 14|en|June 14|qid=Q2612|lt=June 14|1=June 14}} ) – {{ill|Matsudaira Sadanobu|en|Matsudaira Sadanobu|qid=Q284038|lt=Matsudaira Sadanobu|1=Matsudaira Sadanobu}} , senior councilor (rōjū) of the Tokugawa shogunate and architect of the {{ill|Kansei Reforms|ja|Kanseiの改革|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (* {{ill|1758|en|1758|qid=Q7639|lt=1758|1=1758}} ).
== Commemorations ==
* '''Bamboo Drunken Day''' (竹酔日, *Chikusuibi*) – A Chinese folk belief that this day is the best for transplanting bamboo. It is said that the bamboo is "drunk" and unaware of being moved, allowing it to root well. Also known as '''Bamboo Bewilderment Day''' (竹迷日), '''Dragon Birth Day''' (竜生日), or '''Bamboo Birth Day''' (竹誕日).
